name: Carton Volume (m3, litres, ft3)
description: Total carton volume from centimetre dimensions and a carton count, expressed in cubic metres, litres, and cubic feet. The same volume in three units covers sea-freight quoting (m3), warehouse slotting (litres), and US LTL/parcel work (ft3).

inputs.name: Cartons
inputs.description: Number of identical cartons; use 1 for a single carton.

outputs.name: Volume (litres)
outputs.description: Total volume in litres (1 m3 = 1,000 l).

outputs.name: Volume (cubic feet)
outputs.description: Total volume in cubic feet (1 ft3 = 28,316.846592 cm3).

assumptions: Dimensions are outer carton dimensions in centimetres.
All cartons share the same dimensions.
Exact unit conversions: 1 m3 = 1,000,000 cm3 = 1,000 l; 1 ft = 30.48 cm, so 1 ft3 = 28,316.846592 cm3.
limitations: Nominal outer dimensions only; bulging or irregular cartons occupy more space.
Simple sum of carton volumes — no packing, stacking, or honeycombing loss is modelled.
Carriers may round dimensions or volume when rating.

citations.quote: CBM_CENTIMETRES | Cubic metres from cm | `(length_cm x width_cm x height_cm x quantity) / 1,000,000` | cm | m3 ... CUBIC_FEET | Cubic feet from inches | `(length_in x width_in x height_in) / 1728`
